channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Sunday shows preview: Trump jets to Asia amid shutdown; US tensions with Venezuela rising
item.description: As federal workers remain in limbo amid the government shutdown, President Trump is traveling to Asia in an effort to unpack key foreign policy topics at the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) and Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) summits.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens Canada with 10% extra import tax for not pulling down anti-tariffs ad sooner
item.description: President Donald Trump says he plans to hike tariffs on imports of Canadian goods by an extra 10% because of an anti-tariff television ad aired by the province of Ontario.
item.dc:creator: Mark Schiefelbein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump aims to start his Asia trip with dealmaking in Malaysia
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to try to boost his image as an international dealmaker by finalizing a trade agreement with Malaysia and overseeing an expanded ceasefire between Cambodia and Thailand.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, David Rising And Eileen Ng, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro says the US is fabricating a war, seeks to revoke citizenship of opponent López
item.description: CARACAS (AP) — Venezuela’s President Nicolás Maduro said the U.S. government is forging a war against him as the world's biggest warship approached the South American country, while moving to revoke the citizenship of an opponent he accuses of egging on an invasion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Left-leaning independent Connolly wins Irish presidential election
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Left-wing independent Catherine Connolly, who secured the backing of Ireland's left-leaning parties including Sinn Féin, has won the country's presidential election in a landslide victory against her center-right rival.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump travels to Asia and a meeting with China's Xi as the US government shutdown drags on
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia for the first time this term, a trip where he is expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to de-escalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio, Huckabee meet with families of US citizens whose remains are being held hostage in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Ambassador Huckabee met with the families of slain American-Israeli hostages Omer Neutra and Itay Chen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A bomb in Gaza's rubble wounds twins who thought it was a toy
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Shorbasi family was sitting in their severely damaged house in Gaza City, enjoying the relative calm of the ceasefire. Then they heard an explosion and rushed outside to find their 6-year-old twins bleeding on the ground.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warfare expert calls Gaza rebuilding plan 'Disneyland strategy' to defeat Hamas
item.description: Military and Middle East experts say a new Gaza rebuilding plan focused on creating secure enclaves, restoring stability and cutting off Hamas terrorists could succeed in the long term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia kills 4 in overnight strike on Ukraine, days after Trump issues oil sanctions
item.description: Russian strikes on Ukraine overnight left four people dead and several others injured, days after President Trump issued sanctions on Russian oil companies as peace talks in the region have largely been stagnant.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Stephen Miller sidesteps question on US troops in Venezuela as tensions rise
item.description: White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller on Friday dodged a question from reporters about the potential for ground troops on Venezuelan soil, as tensions escalate amid the Trump administration's war on drugs in the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: Russia has grappled with a shrinking and aging population for decades, with President Vladimir Putin viewing it as a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Katie Marie Davies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inspector Clouseau? Mystery man goes viral after Louvre heist
item.description: If the unidentified man really is one of the more than 100 investigators hunting for the jewel thieves, the authorities are keeping it very hush-hush.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Friday the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused Monday. The ad led to Trump calling off trade negotiations between Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Tanzania's first female president has defied early hopes that she would ease repression
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Less than six months after Samia Suluhu Hassan was inaugurated as Tanzania’s first woman president in 2021, the country’s most famous cartoonist depicted her in a military general’s uniform under the title “Dictatoress.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU leaders seek role in Gaza at summit focused on Ukraine and Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are seeking a more active role in Gaza and the occupied West Bank after being sidelined from the U.S.-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: PM Mark Carney says Canada will double its non-US exports
item.description: President Trump has been threatening Canada’s economy and sovereignty with tariffs, most offensively by claiming Canada could be “the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Expert warns critical hours slipping away as kidnappers likely to move US missionary in Niger
item.description: American missionary kidnapped near Niger's presidential palace as security expert warns first 48 hours critical for rescue from ISIS-controlled territory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US carries out 8th strike on alleged drug vessel, this time in Eastern Pacific, Hegseth says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out another strike on a suspected drug vessel, this time in the Eastern Pacific, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre director summoned by lawmakers after jewel theft
item.description: The Louvre is facing questions about lax security measures after thieves made off with priceless jewels.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Kartalija, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine and EU leaders accuse Putin of stalling, reject land concessions for peace
item.description: They accused Russian President Vladimir Putin of stalling for time in diplomatic efforts to bring his invasion of Ukraine to an end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US envoys visit Israel to bolster truce, and Gaza militants hand over a hostage's remains
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Two of U.S. President Donald Trump’s envoys traveled to Israel on Monday to shore up the ceasefire in Gaza, a day after deadly violence gave the fragile deal its first major test.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jane Goodall's cause of death revealed: Report
item.description: Epilepsy was also listed as one of Goodall's conditions, though it's not clear if it contributed to her death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre heist thieves had 'inside help': Former jewel thief
item.description: Lawton described the operation as "very well planned" and explained that before pulling off a heist like this, "you have to know so much.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia recalls US ambassador after Trump's tariff threat, drug remarks
item.description: Trump also called Colombian's leftist President Gustavo Petro an "illegal drug leader" on Sunday, which Petro's government described as offensive.
item.dc:creator: Luis Jaime Acosta and Julia Symmes Cobb / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests US will buy Argentinian beef to bring down prices
item.description: “We would buy some beef from Argentina,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One during a flight from Florida to Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth reveals another US strike on alleged drug-trafficking boat
item.description: According to Hegseth, the strike happened in international waters and the three passengers who were on the vessel were killed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: A cargo aircraft skids off a Hong Kong runway into the sea, killing 2 people
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cargo aircraft skidded off a Hong Kong runway into the sea when landing early Monday, killing two people. The Emirates flight, arriving from Dubai, United Arab Emirates, was landing at Hong Kong International Airport around 3:50 a.m., according to Hong Kong’s airport authority.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thieves steal crown jewels in 4 minutes from Louvre Museum
item.description: PARIS (AP) — In a minutes-long strike Sunday inside the world’s most-visited museum, thieves rode a basket lift up the Louvre ’s facade, forced a window, smashed display cases and fled with priceless Napoleonic jewels, officials said.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, WGN 9
